2007-08-01.title: Television in Afghanistan
2007-08-01.abstract: Owning a TV was a crime under the Taliban. But in a study this year of Afghanistan's five most urban provinces, two-thirds of all people said they watched TV every day or almost every day.

2007-08-01.title: Chinese Market Awash in Fake Potter Books
2007-08-01.abstract: Harry Potter knockoffs abound on the Chinese market, with titles like “Harry Potter and Leopard-Walk-Up-to-Dragon,” and “Harry Potter and the Big Funnel.”

2007-08-01.title: U.N. Approves Peacekeepers for Darfur
2007-08-01.abstract: The resolution will create the world’s largest peacekeeping operation, costing about $2 billion in its first year.

2007-08-01.title: South Korea Urges U.S. to Change Stance on Negotiating With the Taliban
2007-08-01.abstract: South Korea on Tuesday urged the American and Afghan governments to show “flexibility” over Taliban demands for the release of imprisoned militants.

2007-08-01.title: U.S. Death Toll in Iraq in July Expected to Be Lowest in ’07
2007-08-01.abstract: The reduction follows a record 331 fatalities from April to June during intensive military operations in Baghdad and Diyala Province.

2007-08-02.title: Food Shortage in Zimbabwe
2007-08-02.abstract: Zimbabweans try cope with the effect of 10,000-percent-a-year hyperinflation on their food supply.

2007-08-02.title: Russia Set to Plant Flag on Arctic Seabed
2007-08-02.abstract: The Russian research ship Akademik Fyodorov, escorted by a nuclear icebreaker, has reached the North Pole on a mission to send two small piloted submarines to plant a Russian flag on the seabed of the Arctic Ocean, directly under the pole, the Interfax news agency reported. The Mir submarines will dive 15,400 feet, possibly tomorrow, and a member of Parliament will accompany the crew to the bottom. The mission is intended to support Russia’s claim that the Artic Ocean, including the North Pole, lies within Moscow’s exclusive economic zone. Russia maintains that a geological feature called the Lomonosov Ridge, directly under the pole, forms part of the Siberian continental shelf and thus belong to Russia. At issue are scientific projections that the ice cap will recede because of global warming, opening the Artic Ocean to offshore oil drilling. Canada and Denmark also claim the Lomonosov Ridge.

2007-08-02.title: In Mexico, Rebel Politician Courts the Law-and-Order Vote
2007-08-02.abstract: The colorful former mayor of Tijuana, Mexico, stands an even chance of being elected governor of the state of Baja California, despite allegations against him.

2007-08-02.title: West Bank: Israel Starts Razing Wall Near Hebron
2007-08-02.abstract: Israel began dismantling a low concrete barrier that ran for 25 miles along roads south of Hebron. Israel had claimed that the barrier, though just 32 inches high, was intended to provide additional security for Jewish settlers, but in February 2006 the High Court accepted the contention of the Association for Civil Rights in Israel that the wall violated the rights of Palestinian residents in 21 rural communities and prevented farmers from crossing the roads with their herds of livestock. The court gave Israel six months to remove the barrier. When it failed to do so, the civil rights group sought to have the state held in contempt of court. Last week, the court ordered the wall’s destruction within two weeks.

2007-08-02.title: Bosnia Plans to Expel Arabs Who Fought in Its War
2007-08-02.abstract: The Bosnian government, encouraged by Western governments, is concerned that the country should not be seen as a haven for Islamic militants.

2007-08-03.title: French President Chooses U.S. for His Summer Vacation
2007-08-03.abstract: Nicolas Sarkozy plans to spend his summer vacation on the shores of Lake Winnipesaukee in New Hampshire.

2007-08-03.title: Anger Is Tempering Sympathy for South Korean Hostages
2007-08-03.abstract: Sympathy in South Korea for the 21 people remaining in captivity in Afghanistan has been offset by anger over their decision to travel to such a dangerous region.

2007-08-03.title: Gates Offers Blunt Review of Progress in Iraq
2007-08-03.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said that the Bush administration might have misjudged the difficulty of achieving reconciliation between Iraq’s sectarian factions.

2007-08-03.title: Bush Sends Warning Meant for Syria: Don’t Meddle in Lebanon
2007-08-03.abstract: President Bush said Thursday that the United States would freeze the property and assets of anyone trying to undermine Lebanon’s democratically elected government.

2007-08-03.title: Driver in Glasgow Airport Attack Dies From His Burns
2007-08-03.abstract: Kafeel Ahmed, who the British authorities say drove a burning vehicle into an airport terminal in a botched terrorism attack in June, was not expected to survive.

2007-08-04.title: Airplane Crashes Create Anxiety Among Brazil’s Travelers
2007-08-04.abstract: Brazilians are reconsidering their travel options after two major plane crashes in the past 10 months.

2007-08-04.title: A Pakistani Envoy in Britain Defuses Cultural Land Mines
2007-08-04.abstract: Maleeha Lodhi, Pakistan’s high commissioner in Britain, works all angles to convince the British government and public that British Pakistanis are normal people, not terrorists.

2007-08-04.title: Chávez Takes ‘Crazy Battalion’ of Supporters on the Road
2007-08-04.abstract: Using pomp and imagery to court public opinion at home and abroad, Venezuelan President Hugo Chávez has been escorting the actor Sean Penn on a tour around his country.

2007-08-04.title: Night Raid in Iraq: Seeking Militants, but Also Learning the Lay of the Land
2007-08-04.abstract: On a helicopter raid hunting Iraqi insurgents, U.S. troops avoided roadside bombs, established a presence and picked up intelligence.

2007-08-04.title: Marine Sentenced in Killing of Iraqi
2007-08-04.abstract: Sgt. Lawrence G. Hutchins III was sentenced to 15 years in prison for killing an Iraqi man near Hamdaniya last year.

2007-08-05.title: South Asia Grapples With Results of Flooding
2007-08-05.abstract: Unusually heavy monsoons across South Asia this week left a trail of death and disease, testing governments’ ability to cope with heavy floods.

2007-08-05.title: Fate of 5 in U.S. Prisons Weighs on Cubans’ Minds
2007-08-05.abstract: Five Cubans held in the U.S. on espionage charges have become the biggest propaganda tool that Cuba’s Communist government has come up with since Che Guevara.

2007-08-05.title: Brazil Fires Airport Authority President
2007-08-05.abstract: The political fallout from the worst plane crash in Brazilian history continued as the president of the national airports authority was fired.

2007-08-05.title: Iraqi Tries to Bring Sunnis Back to Cabinet
2007-08-05.abstract: President Jalal Talabani was talking to Shiite and Sunni leaders in an effort to resolve a leadership crisis.

2007-08-05.title: Lebanese Election Brings a Tense Showdown
2007-08-05.abstract: An emotional parliamentary election today was widely seen as a bellwether of the country’s political direction.

2007-08-06.title: Jean-Marie Lustiger, French Cardinal, Dies at 80
2007-08-06.abstract: Cardinal Lustiger, who was born to Polish Jews, rose to become leader of the French church and an adviser to Pope John Paul II.

2007-08-06.title: 5 Ministers Threaten to Leave Iraq’s Cabinet
2007-08-06.abstract: The five are all members of the secular Iraqiya coalition led by former Prime Minister Ayad Allawi, a moderate group whose lawmakers are drawn from across sectarian lines.

2007-08-06.title: Iraqi President Starts Talks to Try to Bring Sunnis Back
2007-08-06.abstract: President Jalal Talabani tried on Sunday to find a way to bring Sunnis back into the fold after they walked out of the government last week.

2007-08-06.title: In Rome, a New Ritual on an Old Bridge
2007-08-06.abstract: To some, a tangled mess of padlocks on Rome’s oldest bridge is a symbol of undying love; to others, it is a menace tantamount to vandalism.

2007-08-06.title: Opposition Has Edge in Lebanon Vote
2007-08-06.abstract: A little-known opposition candidate who claimed victory in a parliamentary election could deal a major blow against the Western-backed governing coalition.

2007-08-07.title: ‘Strong Probability’ Outbreak Originated in Labs
2007-08-07.abstract: Viruses that escaped from two veterinary laboratories may have led to an outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease in Britain, a government report said.

2007-08-07.title: To Punish Thai Police, a Hello Kitty Armband
2007-08-07.abstract: A Bangkok police chief has begun punishing wayward police officers by forcing them to wear a pink insignia.

2007-08-07.title: Baron Elie de Rothschild Is Dead at 90
2007-08-07.abstract: Baron Elie oversaw the restoration and ascent of the renowned wine estate Château Lafite Rothschild after World War II.

2007-08-07.title: Climate Change Endangers India, U.N. Says
2007-08-07.abstract: Climate change could destroy vast swaths of farmland in this country, a U.N. official warned today.

2007-08-07.title: China Praises Its Progress Toward Olympics
2007-08-07.abstract: Beijing officials gave an upbeat progress report, even as critics warn that China may fall short on commitments on the environment, human rights and press freedom.

2007-08-08.title: Georgia Accuses Russia of Missile Attack; Moscow Denies It
2007-08-08.abstract: Relations between Russia and Georgia, already strained, have become even more tense because of the accusation.

2007-08-08.title: Tale of Ruling Class Privilege Touches a Nerve in Italians
2007-08-08.abstract: A new book on Italy’s political class has become a phenomenon, catching the wave of a widespread national malaise.

2007-08-08.title: Iran: Another Reformist Paper Closed
2007-08-08.abstract: The authorities shut down Shargh, a leading reformist newspaper, after it published an interview with an Iranian poet who lives in exile and writes about female sexuality, Saghi Ghahraman. It was the third time the paper has been ordered closed, and it was unclear when it might be allowed to resume publishing. The closing was another sign of the increasing pressures on press freedom. In May, the authorities shut down the reformist newspaper Hammihan, and last week, a journalist and blogger, Soheil Assefi, was arrested.

2007-08-08.title: Chris Schwarz, 59, Dies; Photographer Who Honored Polish Jews
2007-08-08.abstract: Mr. Schwarz, a freelance photographer, roamed the ancient heartland of Polish Jewry to record remnants of a disappeared people.

2007-08-08.title: U.S. Says Iran-Supplied Bomb Kills More Troops
2007-08-08.abstract: Attacks on American forces using a lethal type of bomb reached a new high in July, the U.S. military said.

2007-08-09.title: Fossils in Kenya Challenge Linear Evolution
2007-08-09.abstract: Two fossils could rearrange major branches thought to be in a straight ancestral line to Homo sapiens.

2007-08-09.title: Vatican Plays Down Meeting With Priest Accused of Anti-Semitism
2007-08-09.abstract: The Vatican worked to distance itself Thursday from a brief meeting between Pope Benedict XVI and a Polish priest accused of making anti-Semitic statements.

2007-08-09.title: A Korean Bridge Must Span Years of Bias and Sadness
2007-08-09.abstract: In September, a long-anticipated bridge will connect an islet - and a group of people with leprosy - to the Korean mainland for the first time.

2007-08-09.title: Bush Says Iraqi Leader Shares His View on Iran
2007-08-09.abstract: President Bush said today that he was confident that Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki of Iraq shared his view that Iran is a destabilizing force in the region.

2007-08-09.title: Gaza Could Face Economic Meltdown, U.N. Warns
2007-08-09.abstract: The U.N. warned of “disastrous consequences” in Gaza unless the main border crossings into the territory are reopened.

2007-08-10.title: Iraqi Exiles in Jordan
2007-08-10.abstract: Zeinab Majid describes fleeing her war-torn homeland and her new life in Amman.

2007-08-10.title: Iraqi Exiles in Jordan
2007-08-10.abstract: Zeinab Majid describes fleeing her war-torn homeland and her new life in Amman.

2007-08-10.title: France: Bear, Farmers’ Foe, Killed by Car
2007-08-10.abstract: One of five brown bears from Slovenia let loose in the French Pyrenees to increase the native bear population, was killed in a collision with a car.

2007-08-10.title: U.S. Backs Free Elections, Only to See Allies Lose
2007-08-10.abstract: A paradox has frustrated U.S. attempts to bring countries in the Middle East closer to the West through democracy.

2007-08-10.title: Shiite Pilgrims Converging on Baghdad Shrine Are Protected by Improved Security
2007-08-10.abstract: Pilgrims said that this year, security had improved, with a strict curfew in place and Iraqi and American forces guarding a well-defined route.

2007-08-11.title: A Desire to Feed the World and Inspire Self-Sufficiency
2007-08-11.abstract: Once a major figure in the Rev. Sun Myung Moon’s Unification Church and a Bush administration official, Josette Sheeran is now settling in as executive director of the World Food Program.

2007-08-11.abstract: The city of Baghdad has hired two dozen artists to paint murals upon the miles of vast concrete blast walls that now snake along roads, rivers and sidewalks throughout the city.

2007-08-11.title: Concern Rises About Reports of New Fighting in Darfur
2007-08-11.abstract: More than 100 soldiers may have been killed in fighting between Sudanese government troops and rebels in Darfur, an African Union spokesman said.

2007-08-11.title: A Segregated Road in an Already Divided Land
2007-08-11.abstract: The Israeli side of a divided road being built through the West Bank has various exits; the Palestinian side bypasses Jerusalem.

2007-08-11.title: To Curb Illegal Migration, Spain Offers a Legal Route
2007-08-11.abstract: Hoping to reduce the number of Africans making risky boat trips to reach Europe for jobs, Spain has launched a new plan offering legal passage and work permits.

2007-08-12.title: Matador Wins. Bull Dies. The End? Not in Portugal.
2007-08-12.abstract: A heavy fine on one of Portugal’s most famous matadors has ignited a debate on the country’s 80-year ban on killing the loser in bullfights.

2007-08-12.abstract: Amid continuing tension between Palestinian factions, at least 20 people were wounded Saturday in northern Gaza as Hamas forces broke up a demonstration by Fatah supporters.

2007-08-12.title: Millions Flock to Vote in Sierra Leone
2007-08-12.abstract: It is the first election in Sierra Leone since United Nations peacekeeping efforts ended two years ago.

2007-08-12.title: East German Shoot-to-Kill Order Is Found
2007-08-12.abstract: It is the first written proof that border guards were told to shoot to kill at anyone trying to escape to West Germany.

2007-08-12.title: In City of Ancient Bridges, Dissent Over a New One
2007-08-12.abstract: A new bridge over Venice’s Grand Canal, the first to be built in 73 years, is stirring dissent for its cost and modern design.

2007-08-13.title: Somalia and Ethiopia Are Accused of War Crimes
2007-08-13.abstract: Human Rights Watch  accused the transitional government in Somalia and the Ethiopian troops that helped bring it to power of committing war crimes in Somalia.

2007-08-13.title: China, Filling a Void, Drills for Riches in Chad
2007-08-13.abstract: Chinese companies are investing heavily in impoverished African countries like Chad, raising Western concerns.

2007-08-13.abstract: Envisioned as a “Pittsburgh of the tropics,”  Ciudad Guayana, Venezuela, didn’t turn out quite as planned, and that’s casting doubts on President Hugo Chávez’s city-building ambitions.

2007-08-13.abstract: Hoping to forge a compromise among warring factions, Nuri al-Maliki said Sunday that he would meet as early as this week with leaders from Iraq’s major political parties.

2007-08-13.title: Troops Shelter an Unlikely Survivor in Baghdad
2007-08-13.abstract: A 9-month-old girl is recovering in an American military hospital after a death squad killed her mother and uncle.

2007-08-14.title: Low-Cost Canal Living
2007-08-14.abstract: Amsterdam's canals are home to hundreds who have elected to live on houseboats.

2007-08-14.title: A First Lady’s No-Show Is Seen as a No-No
2007-08-14.abstract: Cécilia Sarkozy is under scrutiny for ducking out of public appearances where a presidential spouse would be expected.

2007-08-14.abstract: Human Rights Watch said that Ethiopian troops had shelled hospitals, that Somali officials had blocked aid convoys and that both forces had shown wanton indifference toward civilians.

2007-08-15.title: Italy: Refugee Bodies Seen Off Coast
2007-08-15.abstract: The Coast Guard reported spotting as many as 14 bodies floating in the sea south of Sicily.

2007-08-15.title: U.S. Set to Declare Iran Guards Terrorists
2007-08-15.abstract: A White House spokeswoman today deflected questions about the designation for Iran’s Revolutionary Guard.

2007-08-16.abstract: President Hugo Chávez said the changes would affect “less than 10 percent” of the Constitution but would bring Venezuela “new horizons for the new era.”

2007-08-17.abstract: The primordial rocks in Hyderabad, India, have become the focus of one of India’s many emerging citizens movements.

2007-08-17.abstract: Iran lashed out at American plans for a missile defense shield, while Russia took an indirect swipe at what it called Washington’s unilateral foreign policy.

2007-08-17.abstract: A report by a panel of international military experts stopped short of directly implicating Russia in the matter.

2007-08-18.title: Entrepreneurs From China Flourish in Africa
2007-08-18.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of Chinese migrants are doing business on a continent that until five years or so ago had been terra incognita.

2007-08-18.abstract: Beijing city officials began four days of driving restrictions to determine the impact on air quality and traffic flow.

2007-08-19.abstract: Six hundred troops are being sent to stop looting in Peru’s southern region that was devastated by Wednesday’s earthquake,  while aftershocks continue and relief efforts increase.

2007-08-19.abstract: Western Iraq’s improved security, often cited as a success story, could be undermined as the Marines withdraw.

2007-08-19.abstract: An influx of immigrants is helping Ireland’s population grow rapidly and escape the “graying” fate of the rest of Europe.

2007-08-20.abstract: The British government is financing an effort to teach a special curriculum intended to reach students who might be vulnerable to Islamic extremism.

2007-08-20.abstract: A former mayor of Seoul, Lee Myung-bak, immediately became the front-runner in the South Korean presidential election.

2007-08-21.abstract: The party of Nursultan A. Nazarbayev, the authoritarian president who has been in power since the Soviet era, trounced all competitors in parliamentary elections over the weekend, taking all 98 contested seats.

2007-08-21.abstract: Hurricane Dean gained strength on Monday, turning into a Category 5 hurricane as it surged past the Cayman Islands and directly toward Mexico.

2007-08-21.abstract: Hurricane Dean’s expected landfall on the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico threw into doubt three-way talks that were scheduled for Tuesday.

2007-08-22.abstract: The White House is trying to distance itself from the Iraqi prime minister before President Bush’s troop buildup is re-evaluated.

2007-08-22.title: Peru Family’s Struggle Reflects Hardships After Quake
2007-08-22.abstract: Despite foreign aid arriving, Peruvians throughout the earthquake-ravaged zone are struggling to get the basic food and water they need to survive.

2007-08-22.abstract: The known casualty toll from a series of truck bombs near the Syrian border has soared to more than 500 dead.

2007-08-22.abstract: A Jewish physician from Hawaii personally delivered 15 new surfboards to Palestinian surfing enthusiasts in Gaza.

2007-08-22.abstract: Hurricane Dean began battering Mexico’s Gulf coast as residents fled or hunkered down at home.

2007-08-23.title: Abbas Criticizes Israeli Strikes in Gaza
2007-08-23.abstract: The Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas, condemned Israel for what he termed an escalation of violence against Palestinians.

2007-08-23.abstract: Less than half the number of blast-resistant trucks will be delivered to Iraq by year’s end than planned because of the time it takes to equip them with radios and armaments and then deliver them by sea.

2007-08-23.abstract: Many historians disagree with President Bush’s reference to the Vietnam war as a predictor of what might happen in Iraq should the United States withdraw.

2007-08-23.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel’s coalition government of conservatives and Social Democrats will gather Thursday to seek unity and direction.

2007-08-23.abstract: Haleh Esfandiari’s lawyer says that Iranian authorities still hold the scholar’s passport, but that by Iranian law, she should be permitted to leave Iran.

2007-08-24.abstract: A federal judge in Miami cleared the way for the former Panamanian leader to face money laundering charges.

2007-08-24.abstract: Today’s anti-Hamas demonstration was the largest since the Islamic militant group seized control of Gaza after a week of factional fighting in June.

2007-08-24.abstract: The chief of the federal prosecution service’s investigations committee said that the killing of Anna Politkovskaya, the journalist who was fatally shot in Moscow last year, might soon be solved.

2007-08-25.abstract: Members of the Hamas Executive Force broke up a demonstration by Fatah in Gaza City, firing into the air and briefly detaining journalists.

2007-08-25.abstract: American soldiers battled Shiite militiamen in a poor neighborhood in northern Baghdad early on Friday, firing from helicopters and killing 18 gunmen.

2007-08-25.abstract: Families of Fatah al Islam militants holed up in the battered Palestinian refugee camp Nahr al Bared were permitted to leave under a temporary truce with the army.

2007-08-25.abstract: Pakistan’s president, Gen. Pervez Musharraf, said there was a need for dialogue to ensure a smooth period of preparation for elections.

2007-08-25.abstract: Two Palestinian gunmen entered Israel from the Gaza Strip and moved nearly half a mile into Israel before soldiers killed them.

2007-08-26.abstract: A pair of synchronized explosions tore through two popular spots in Hyderabad, killing at least 30 people and wounding 60 others.

2007-08-26.abstract: Delays in the trial of Charles Taylor have caused much finger-pointing at the U.N.-backed special court.

2007-08-26.abstract: Various European officials are trumpeting a substantial drop in the number of African migrants crossing the Mediterranean.

2007-08-26.abstract: The Gdansk shipyard was the epicenter of Poland’s Solidarity movement in the 1980s.  Today it is facing an uncertain future as it struggles with potential bankruptcy and a changing industrial landscape.

2007-08-27.title: Changing Lifestyles in the Kibbutz
2007-08-27.abstract: Once bastions of Marxist collective living, Israel's kibbutzim are once again thriving, but with a mellower, suburbanized version of socialism.

2007-08-27.title: Julius Wess, 72, Theoretical Physicist, Is Dead
2007-08-27.abstract: Dr. Wess, along with Bruno Zumino, introduced the idea of supersymmetry among fundamental particles, which may help explain the universe’s missing dark matter.

2007-08-27.title: Governor of Iran’s Central Bank Resigns
2007-08-27.abstract: This month, three key economic figures from the cabinet of Iran’s President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad.

2007-08-27.title: Russia Arrests 10 in Journalist Death
2007-08-27.abstract: A Chechen crime boss and career officers from the police and intelligence services were among those arrested for roles in the contract killing of Anna Politkovskaya.

2007-08-27.title: Japanese Premier Reshuffles Cabinet
2007-08-27.abstract: With his approval ratings sinking, Prime Minister Shinzo Abe today formed a new cabinet made up of veterans.

2007-08-28.title: Tuesday’s Expert: Yang Fuqiang
2007-08-28.abstract: Welcome to the second day our roundtable discussion of China’s environment, part of our ongoing series, “Choking on Growth.” Throughout the week, a panel of scholars and environmentalists will take turns answering your questions on the political, social, scientific and economic challenges posed by the country’s rapid industrialization and equally sudden pollution crisis.

2007-08-28.title: 50 Killed at Religious Festival in Iraq
2007-08-28.abstract: A power struggle between rival Shiite groups erupted during a religious festival in Karbala today, as gunmen with machine guns and rocket-propelled grenades fought street battles amid crowds of pilgrims, killing 50 people and wounding 200, Iraqi officials said.

2007-08-28.title: Kosovo Talks to Start Anew, but Parties Express Doubt
2007-08-28.abstract: Leading members of the opposing delegations in talks over Kosovo’s future, which begin Wednesday, said that they doubted that a compromise could be reached.

2007-08-28.title: A Religious Candidate Is Ascendant in Turkey
2007-08-28.abstract: Abdullah Gul, a religious man in the secular realm of Turkish politics, hopes to persuade the establishment that it has nothing to fear from his candidacy.

2007-08-28.title: U.N. Leader to Travel to Sudan Area
2007-08-28.abstract: Secretary General Ban Ki-moon announced  that he would travel to Sudan, Chad and Libya  to press for an end to fresh violence in Darfur.

2007-08-29.title: Violence in Karbala
2007-08-29.abstract: Fighting erupted between two rival Shiite groups during a religious festival in Karbala, Iraq.

2007-08-29.title: Violence in Karbala
2007-08-29.abstract: Fighting erupted between two rival Shiite groups during a religious festival in Karbala, Iraq.

2007-08-29.title: Plan Released by Iran and U.N. Atomic Agency Is Faulted
2007-08-29.abstract: An agreement between Iran and the U.N. nuclear agency is inadequate and is likely to delay further international sanctions against the country, some say.

2007-08-29.title: U.S. Releases Group of Iranians Held in Baghdad
2007-08-29.abstract: Members of an Iranian delegation visiting Baghdad at the invitation of the Iraqi government were released Wednesday, a day after being arrested at their hotel by American troops

2007-08-29.title: Close Political Ally of Brazilian President to Face Corruption Trial
2007-08-29.abstract: José Dirceu de Oliveira e Silva, whom many call the architect behind the Brazilian president’s rise to power, resigned in 2005 after being accused of vote-buying.

2007-08-30.title: Shiite Festival in Iran
2007-08-30.abstract: On the birthday of Imam Mahdi, Iranians celebrate the savior of the Shiite faith.

2007-08-30.title: Pakistani Leader to Exit Army Job, His Rival Reports
2007-08-30.abstract: Gen. Pervez Musharraf would resign as army chief and serve another term as president and former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto would return to Pakistan.

2007-08-30.title: Continuing Myanmar Protests Defy Junta
2007-08-30.abstract: The small street protests over the last two weeks are the most sustained challenge to the junta in a decade.

2007-08-30.title: Iran Expanding Its Nuclear Program, Agency Reports
2007-08-30.abstract: Iran is expanding its nuclear program in defiance of United Nations’ resolutions, the International Atomic Energy Agency said Thursday.

2007-08-30.title: White House Is Gaining Confidence It Can Win Fight in Congress Over Iraq Policy
2007-08-30.abstract: Current and former administration officials say the president’s hand is stronger now than it was in early July, when a string of Republican defections had many worried.

2007-08-31.title: Iraq's Search for Reconciliation
2007-08-31.abstract: Shatha al-Musawi, a Shiite lawmaker, is struggling to move past her difficult history in order to help Iraq move forward.

2007-08-31.title: Decades After War Trials, Japan Still Honors a Dissenting Judge
2007-08-31.abstract: Radhabinod Pal, the only one out of 11 Allied justices who handed down a not guilty verdict for Japan’s top wartime leaders at the Tokyo trials, is still big in Japan.

2007-08-31.title: Rights Group Accuses Hezbollah of Indiscriminate Attacks on Civilians in Israel War
2007-08-31.abstract: Lebanese leaders have joined together to condemn Human Rights Watch, accusing it of blaming the victims of the conflict.

2007-08-31.title: Report Showing Rise in Iran’s Nuclear Activity Exposes Split Between U.S. and U.N.
2007-08-31.abstract: The I.A.E.A. report has exposed a new divide between the United Nations inspectors and the United States over how to contain Iran’s atomic program.

2007-08-31.title: Shiite’s Tale: How Gulf With Sunnis Widened
2007-08-31.abstract: For Shatha al-Musawi, a Shiite member of Parliament in Iraq, the urge to reconcile is being blacked out by distrust.
